title: Fold-safe target encodings for categorical strategy priors
summary: Add smoothed out-of-fold target encodings for race, driver, compound, and selected interactions, implemented outside generic preprocessing to avoid leakage.
body:
Evidence from artifacts:
- Strong baseline variants use frequency encodings and group medians for Driver, Race, Compound, and Race_Year.
- No successful node appears to use proper out-of-fold target encoding.
- Several failures were caught by leakage guards around PitNextLap, so target-derived features need explicit fold-safe handling.

Why this might improve ROC AUC:
Pit strategy contains stable categorical priors. Race, driver, compound, year, and interactions can encode event propensity that frequency encoding cannot capture.

Concrete implementation plan:
- Applies first to legacy/manual or a custom AutoGluon wrapper.
- Do not implement inside a generic preprocess(df) function.
- Build OOF smoothed target encodings for Driver, Race, Compound, Race_Year, Race_Year x Compound, and Driver x Compound.
- Use global-prior smoothing and minimum-count thresholds.
- For validation rows, encodings must use fold-training labels only.
- For test rows, fit encoders on all training labels.
- Feed encoded columns to AutoGluon or manual GBDT.

Leakage and validation risks:
- High leakage risk if implemented naively.
- Never use validation labels for validation encodings.
- Group CV may give a more realistic estimate than random CV for race-level encodings.

Expected score impact:
- Impact: medium.
- Confidence: medium-low.
- Speculative due implementation risk.

Fast experiment design:
- Minimal change: add one OOF-smoothed Race_Year x Compound encoding.
- Confirm if grouped CV and worst-fold AUC improve.
- Reject if only random CV improves while group CV falls.

Prompt snippet for AIDE coding agent:
Implement fold-safe smoothed target encoding outside preprocess: generate OOF encodings for Race_Year, Compound, and Race_Year x Compound, use fold-training labels only, and feed those columns into the existing step-65 feature/model pipeline.
